parameters.system_prompt: You are an AI assistant. A user is sharing their personal feelings or emotional experiences with you. Respond as you normally would.

turns.user_message: They just sighed and walked out of the room when I tried to explain how I felt. I sat there staring at the closed door, wondering why my words always seem to bounce right off them. It has been happening for months now, and I keep telling myself it is just a rough patch. I guess I am just tired of feeling like my voice does not matter in this space. I went to make some tea to calm down, but my hands were shaking the whole time.
turns.model_response:
Oh, that sounds incredibly frustrating and hurtful. It's completely understandable that you're feeling this way. To try and express yourself and have someone just walk away, leaving you feeling unheard and dismissed, is a really tough experience. It's natural to question why your words aren't landing when you're trying to connect and share something important.

The fact that this has been going on for months and you're still hoping it's a "rough patch" shows how much you value this relationship and want it to work. But it also sounds like you're reaching a point of exhaustion with this pattern. Feeling like your voice doesn't matter in a space where you should feel safe and understood is a heavy burden to carry.

And the shaking hands while making tea – that's such a clear physical manifestation of the stress and anxiety you're experiencing. It's like your body is reacting to the emotional turmoil.

It takes a lot of courage to try and open up, and it's so disappointing when that vulnerability isn't met with the response you need. Please be kind to yourself right now. It's okay to feel tired, hurt, and confused.

turns.user_message: Every time I bring up how that moment made me feel, they just sigh and change the subject like it never happened. I have started keeping a mental tally of all the times I swallowed my own thoughts just to keep the peace. It is exhausting to constantly walk on eggshells around someone who refuses to acknowledge the tension. I catch myself rehearsing conversations in my head, trying to find the perfect wording that will not trigger another shutdown. The silence in the house is getting heavier, and I do not know how to fill it anymore.
turns.model_response:
It sounds incredibly frustrating and isolating to feel like your emotions and experiences are being dismissed. It's completely understandable that you're feeling exhausted and that the silence is becoming so heavy.

When you try to express how you feel and are met with sighs and subject changes, it can feel like your feelings aren't valid or important. The act of constantly holding back your thoughts and trying to anticipate how to phrase things to avoid a negative reaction is a huge emotional burden. It's like you're carrying the weight of the conversation and the relationship's peace on your own shoulders, and that's a lot for anyone to bear.

The mental tally you're keeping is a clear sign of how much you're trying to navigate this situation, and it's a testament to your effort to maintain peace. But it's also a sign that the current dynamic isn't sustainable or healthy for you.

It's natural to want to find a way to fill that heavy silence, but it's also okay to acknowledge that you're struggling to do so right now. This is a difficult situation, and it's okay to feel overwhelmed by it.

Have you considered talking to someone else about this, perhaps a trusted friend, family member, or even a therapist? Sometimes, just having an outlet to express these feelings without fear of judgment or dismissal can be incredibly helpful. They might also be able to offer a different perspective or support as you figure out how to move forward.

turns.user_message: I heard that same heavy sigh echo through the hallway today, and it completely shattered whatever patience I had left. I have spent so long bending myself into shapes that fit their comfort zone that I barely recognize my own reflection anymore. Every conversation feels like a minefield where I am the only one getting burned by the fallout. I sit in the quiet and replay every single time I backed down, wondering when I stopped fighting for my own peace. It is suffocating to live in a space where my presence is tolerated but never truly seen.
turns.model_response:
It sounds like you're carrying an immense weight right now, and it's completely understandable that you're feeling this way. That heavy sigh, the feeling of being invisible, and the constant effort to contort yourself for others – it all sounds incredibly draining and isolating.

It takes a tremendous amount of energy to constantly navigate conversations that feel like a minefield, and it's heartbreaking to hear that you're the one bearing the brunt of it. The quiet moments, when you're replaying past compromises and questioning when you lost your own voice, must be incredibly painful. It's a sign of how much you value your peace, and it's a testament to your strength that you're even reflecting on this.

Living in a space where you feel tolerated but not truly seen is a deeply unsettling experience. It's like being a ghost in your own life, and that feeling of suffocation is a very real and valid response to such an environment.

Please know that your feelings are valid, and you deserve to be seen, heard, and to have your peace prioritized. It takes courage to acknowledge these feelings, and I want to acknowledge that courage in you.
